factors.summary: Overall demand is stable, with flat projections for industry volumes in both North America and Rest of World.

evidence.text: In 2024, we recognized restructuring and impairment expenses of $17.6 million. In China, severance expenses of $11.3 million related to the right sizing of that business for current market conditions. The remaining $6.3 million related to the restructuring of our water treatment business in North America as a part of a profitability improvement strategy that prioritizes improving our cost structure and emphasizes our more profitable channels.

factors.summary: Order backlog or book-to-bill is stable, with no significant changes mentioned.

evidence.text: Our sales in the first quarter of 2025 were $963.9 million, and lower than the first quarter of 2024 sales of $978.8 million. Compared to the prior year quarter, our net sales decrease was primarily driven by lower residential and commercial water heater volumes in North America, lower sales in China, and an unfavorable currency translation of approximately $7 million due to the depreciation of foreign currencies compared to the U.S. dollar, which more than offset our higher sales of boilers and incremental sales related to the 2024 acquisition of Pureit that added approximately $12 million in the first quarter.

factors.summary: The company has announced price increases, but the impact is not yet fully realized due to the uncertainty of the tariff environment.

evidence.text: Given the uncertainty of the tariff environment, our guidance does not include our announced pricing which we project will offset, along with other actions, the current announced tariffs through the date of this filing. In addition to pricing, our other actions include footprint optimization, strategic sourcing actions and other cost containment initiatives.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin products such as boilers, with a focus on more profitable channels, which is beneficial.

evidence.text: We anticipate that commercial water heater industry volumes will also be approximately flat in 2025 after minimal growth in 2024. In response to higher steel and other input costs, including tariffs, we announced price increases on most of our water heater and boiler products. We expect our boiler sales to grow between three and five percent in 2025 compared to 2024 as we continue to benefit from the transition to higher efficiency boilers.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is flat to up two percent compared to 2024, driven by stable industry volumes and cost management.

evidence.text: Combining all of these factors, we expect our 2025 consolidated sales to be approximately flat to up two percent compared to 2024. Given the uncertainty of the tariff environment, our guidance does not include our announced pricing which we project will offset, along with other actions, the current announced tariffs through the date of this filing. In addition to pricing, our other actions include footprint optimization, strategic sourcing actions and other cost containment initiatives. Our guidance also excludes the impacts from potential future acquisitions.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are stable in North America and declining in China, driven by market conditions and demand softness.

evidence.text: In our North America segment, we saw lower water heater volumes in the first quarter of 2025 against a difficult comparison in the same period last year. We believe that a pre-buy ahead of our March 2024 price increase pulled forward some demand into the first quarter of 2024. 2024 residential industry unit volumes were flat compared to the prior year and we project 2025 industry residential unit volumes will be flat as well. In the first quarter of 2025, our Board of Directors approved adding 5,000,000 shares of common stock to the existing discretionary share repurchase authority.

factors.summary: The North America market is stable with flat volumes, while the Rest of World, particularly China, is experiencing demand softness.

evidence.text: In our Rest of World segment, our first quarter 2025 third-party sales in China declined four percent due to continued weak consumer demand. In 2025, we project our third-party sales in China to decrease between five to eight percent in local currency compared to 2024 as we expect consumer demand softness will persist in 2025.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, particularly steel and other materials, which are being partially offset by price increases.

evidence.text: In response to higher steel and other input costs, including tariffs, we announced price increases on most of our water heater and boiler products. We expect our boiler sales to grow between three and five percent in 2025 compared to 2024 as we continue to benefit from the transition to higher efficiency boilers.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, with restructuring expenses in China and North America indicating ongoing cost management efforts.

evidence.text: In China, severance expenses of $11.3 million related to the right sizing of that business for current market conditions. The remaining $6.3 million related to the restructuring of our water treatment business in North America as a part of a profitability improvement strategy that prioritizes improving our cost structure and emphasizes our more profitable channels.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are declining slightly due to lower sales volumes and higher interest expenses.

evidence.text: Our gross profit margin in the first quarter of 2025 was 38.9 percent, down compared to 39.3 percent in the first quarter of 2024. The decrease in gross profit margin was primarily due to lower sales volumes. Selling, general, and administrative expenses in the first quarter of 2025 were essentially flat compared to the first quarter of 2024. Interest expense in the first quarter of 2025 was $2.9 million compared to $1.0 million in the same period last year. The increase in interest expense in the first quarter of 2025 was primarily due to higher debt levels.

factors.summary: Restructuring and impairment actions are delivering measurable savings, particularly in China and North America.

evidence.text: In China, severance expenses of $11.3 million related to the right sizing of that business for current market conditions. The remaining $6.3 million related to the restructuring of our water treatment business in North America as a part of a profitability improvement strategy that prioritizes improving our cost structure and emphasizes our more profitable channels.

factors.summary: Productivity improvements are evident through cost controlling measures and benefits from restructuring actions in the Rest of World segment.

evidence.text: The higher segment earnings and segment margin in 2025 compared to last year were primarily driven by cost controlling measures and benefits from our Q4 2024 restructuring actions which more than offset lower volumes in China.

factors.summary: Supply chain health is stable, with no significant disruptions mentioned.

evidence.text: Our working capital was $540.0 million at March 31, 2025, compared with $495.7 million at December 31, 2024. The increase in working capital was primarily related to higher receivable balances, lower accounts payable and lower payroll-related accruals and was partially offset by lower cash balances.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is increasing in North America in preparation for new efficiency rules, signaling confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: In addition, we are expanding our commercial water heater capacity in North America in preparation for the new efficiency rule for commercial water heaters that the Department of Energy (DOE) has adopted that will take effect in 2026.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are stable, with no significant build-up or drawdown mentioned.

evidence.text: Our working capital was $540.0 million at March 31, 2025, compared with $495.7 million at December 31, 2024. The increase in working capital was primarily related to higher receivable balances, lower accounts payable and lower payroll-related accruals and was partially offset by lower cash balances.

factors.summary: No significant quality or safety incidents are mentioned.

evidence.text: Our sales in the first quarter of 2025 were $963.9 million, and lower than the first quarter of 2024 sales of $978.8 million. Compared to the prior year quarter, our net sales decrease was primarily driven by lower residential and commercial water heater volumes in North America, lower sales in China, and an unfavorable currency translation of approximately $7 million due to the depreciation of foreign currencies compared to the U.S. dollar, which more than offset our higher sales of boilers and incremental sales related to the 2024 acquisition of Pureit that added approximately $12 million in the first quarter.

factors.summary: Management has maintained forward guidance for revenue, with projections for flat to up two percent growth, and has not lowered expectations.

evidence.text: Combining all of these factors, we expect our 2025 consolidated sales to be approximately flat to up two percent compared to 2024. Given the uncertainty of the tariff environment, our guidance does not include our announced pricing which we project will offset, along with other actions, the current announced tariffs through the date of this filing. In addition to pricing, our other actions include footprint optimization, strategic sourcing actions and other cost containment initiatives. Our guidance also excludes the impacts from potential future acquisitions.

factors.summary: Management is cautious due to economic conditions and uncertainties in the tariff environment, but remains confident in their strategies.

evidence.text: Given the uncertainty of the tariff environment, our guidance does not include our announced pricing which we project will offset, along with other actions, the current announced tariffs through the date of this filing. In addition to pricing, our other actions include footprint optimization, strategic sourcing actions and other cost containment initiatives. Our guidance also excludes the impacts from potential future acquisitions.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include continued weak demand in China, ongoing tariff uncertainties, and higher input costs.

evidence.text: In our Rest of World segment, our first quarter 2025 third-party sales in China declined four percent due to continued weak consumer demand. In 2025, we project our third-party sales in China to decrease between five to eight percent in local currency compared to 2024 as we expect consumer demand softness will persist in 2025. The change in the effective income tax rate for the three months ended March 31, 2025 compared to the prior year quarter was primarily due to a change in geographical earnings mix.

factors.summary: Long-term strategy is focused on geographic expansion, product diversification, and cost management to drive growth.

evidence.text: We continue to seek acquisitions that enable geographic growth, expand our core business, and establish adjacencies. On November 1, 2024, we acquired Pureit from Unilever for approximately $125 million, subject to customary adjustments. Pureit, a leading water purification business in South Asia, offers a broad range of residential water purification solutions. Pureit contributed $12 million to sales in the first quarter of 2025 and is expected to have annual sales of approximately USD $50 million for the full year 2025. The acquisition fits squarely in our core capabilities and doubles our market penetration in the South Asia region.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include commodity prices, interest rates, and foreign exchange movements, which could significantly impact near-term results.

evidence.text: The change in the effective income tax rate for the three months ended March 31, 2025 compared to the prior year quarter was primarily due to a change in geographical earnings mix. We estimate that our annual effective income tax rate for the full year of 2025 will be approximately 24 to 24.5 percent.

factors.summary: New product launches, such as gas tankless water heaters, and successful integration of acquisitions like Pureit are expected to drive future growth.

evidence.text: We also recently introduced our internally designed and manufactured gas tankless water heaters in North America. In addition, we are expanding our commercial water heater capacity in North America in preparation for the new efficiency rule for commercial water heaters that the Department of Energy (DOE) has adopted that will take effect in 2026. On November 1, 2024, we acquired Pureit from Unilever for approximately $125 million, subject to customary adjustments. Pureit, a leading water purification business in South Asia, offers a broad range of residential water purification solutions. Pureit contributed $12 million to sales in the first quarter of 2025 and is expected to have annual sales of approximately USD $50 million for the full year 2025. The acquisition fits squarely in our core capabilities and doubles our market penetration in the South Asia region.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are a mixed bag, with stable demand in North America but weak demand in China, and overall uncertainty due to tariffs and inflation.

evidence.text: In our Rest of World segment, our first quarter 2025 third-party sales in China declined four percent due to continued weak consumer demand. In 2025, we project our third-party sales in China to decrease between five to eight percent in local currency compared to 2024 as we expect consumer demand softness will persist in 2025. The change in the effective income tax rate for the three months ended March 31, 2025 compared to the prior year quarter was primarily due to a change in geographical earnings mix.

factors.summary: Interest rates have increased, leading to higher debt service costs, which is partially offsetting the benefits of other cost containment initiatives.

evidence.text: Interest expense in the first quarter of 2025 was $2.9 million compared to $1.0 million in the same period last year. The increase in interest expense in the first quarter of 2025 was primarily due to higher debt levels.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are unfavorably impacting results, particularly in China, due to currency depreciation affecting sales.

evidence.text: Our sales in the first quarter of 2025 were $963.9 million, and lower than the first quarter of 2024 sales of $978.8 million. Compared to the prior year quarter, our net sales decrease was primarily driven by lower residential and commercial water heater volumes in North America, lower sales in China, and an unfavorable currency translation of approximately $7 million due to the depreciation of foreign currencies compared to the U.S. dollar, which more than offset our higher sales of boilers and incremental sales related to the 2024 acquisition of Pureit that added approximately $12 million in the first quarter.

factors.summary: Geopolitical risks, such as trade tensions and conflicts, are identified as potential headwinds, particularly in relation to tariffs and trade restrictions.

evidence.text: Given the uncertainty of the tariff environment, our guidance does not include our announced pricing which we project will offset, along with other actions, the current announced tariffs through the date of this filing. In addition to pricing, our other actions include footprint optimization, strategic sourcing actions and other cost containment initiatives.

factors.summary: CapEx is expected to remain steady, with projections of $90 million to $100 million for 2025, signaling confidence in future growth opportunities.

evidence.text: We project 2025 capital expenditures will be between $90 million and $100 million and full-year depreciation and amortization expense will be approximately $80 million.

factors.summary: Debt leverage has increased due to stock repurchases, but the company remains in compliance with financial covenants.

evidence.text: Our total debt increased by $76.6 million in the first three months of 2025 as we used available cash to fund our stock repurchase program. Our leverage, as measured by the ratio of total debt to total capitalization, was 12.7 percent at March 31, 2025, compared with 9.3 percent at December 31, 2024.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are active, with a stock repurchase program and dividend payments, indicating management's confidence in the company's future.

evidence.text: Depending on factors such as stock price, working capital requirements, and alternative investment opportunities, we expect to spend approximately $400 million on stock repurchases in 2025 through a combination of any Rule 10b5-1 automatic trading plan and open market repurchases. On April 7, 2025, our Board of Directors declared a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.34 per share on our Common Stock and Class A common stock. The dividend is payable on May 15, 2025, to shareholders of record on April 30, 2025.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is decreasing due to lower operating cash flows and higher capital expenditures, indicating a focus on maintaining liquidity.

evidence.text: Cash provided by operations in the first three months of 2025 was $38.7 million and lower than $106.6 million in the first three months of 2024, primarily due to lower accounts receivable collections which resulted from lower prior quarter sales as well as lower current quarter earnings. Our free cash flow in the first quarter of 2025 and 2024 was $17.4 million and $84.6 million, respectively. We expect cash provided by operating activities to be between $600 million and $650 million in 2025. We expect free cash flow to be between $500 million and $550 million in 2025. Free cash flow is a non-GAAP measure described in more detail in the Non-GAAP Measures section below.

factors.summary: M&A activity is focused on geographic expansion and product diversification, with recent acquisitions like Pureit contributing positively to sales.

evidence.text: On November 1, 2024, we acquired Pureit from Unilever for approximately $125 million, subject to customary adjustments. Pureit, a leading water purification business in South Asia, offers a broad range of residential water purification solutions. Pureit contributed $12 million to sales in the first quarter of 2025 and is expected to have annual sales of approximately USD $50 million for the full year 2025. The acquisition fits squarely in our core capabilities and doubles our market penetration in the South Asia region.

factors.summary: Regulatory changes, particularly new efficiency rules for commercial water heaters, are driving capacity expansion and strategic investments.

evidence.text: In addition, we are expanding our commercial water heater capacity in North America in preparation for the new efficiency rule for commercial water heaters that the Department of Energy (DOE) has adopted that will take effect in 2026.

evidence.text: We expect our boiler sales to grow between three and five percent in 2025 compared to 2024 as we continue to benefit from the transition to higher efficiency boilers.

evidence.text: In response to higher steel and other input costs, including tariffs, we announced price increases on most of our water heater and boiler products. We expect our boiler sales to grow between three and five percent in 2025 compared to 2024 as we continue to benefit from the transition to higher efficiency boilers.
